text: The surgical team establishes initial access at the umbilicus using a twelve millimeter robotic port, followed by the placement of three additional ports targeting the epigastrium. The assistant port and first robotic port are positioned in the midclavicular line, with the assistant port placed caudally to minimize instrument collisions.
Under laparoscopic visualization, the surgeon guides the robotic instruments through the placed trochars into the abdominal cavity.

The surgeon elevates the stomach and creates an anterior gastrotomy using monopolar electrocautery, cutting through the stomach layers until the mucosa is visible. The gastrotomy incision is then extended.

A large protuberance is visible on the posterior wall of the stomach. The surgeon uses intraoperative ultrasound to identify and characterize the collection. Monopolar electrocautery is employed to enter the cavity, evacuating approximately two liters of fluid from the collection.
The surgeon extends the gastrotomy incision to facilitate debridement, removing necrotic tissue from the cavity for extraction at the end of the procedure.

The surgeon performs minimally invasive surgical debridement of extensive necrotic tissue associated with walled-off pancreatic necrosis. Small amounts of tissue that are densely adherent to the cavity wall are left in place, expected to undergo auto-debridement aided by gastric secretions.

The surgeon matures the cyst gastrostomy by suturing the stomach to the cavity wall using resorbable barbed sutures in a running fashion. Two running sutures are anchored at the apices of the cyst gastrostomy.

The surgeon closes the anterior gastrotomy in a single layer using a resorbable barbed suture, taking full thickness bites through the gastric wall to ensure secure closure after the cyst gastrostomy maturation.

The surgeon places necrotic tissue into an endoscopic retrieval bag for removal from the abdomen. Options for ending the suture include tying the tails together using a locking plastic clip or running the sutures past the mid portion of the closure.
The surgeon reinforces the gastrotomy closure using aerosolized fiber and glue, ensuring a secure seal without placing any drains.
